ab: tuahli ku i pakisuahlai ihlau?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: a’ʉ, marumukuaku.
en: B: Yes, I would love to.

ab: tuahli ku i pakisuahlai ihlau?
en: A:Can I interview you?

ab: malivutu, maini acahli ku.
en: B:Don’t, I don’t know much about it.

ab: maru i pasamia u kʉlʉpʉngʉ kumamahlipapu? hlatumua ku mahlukua ihlau takuliliungu tuahlʉ i?
ch: A:你下午有空嗎?我想去拜訪你可以嗎?
en: A: Are you free this afternoon? I’d like to visit you, is that okay?

ab: a’ʉ, ma ku salia upapalu ihlau.
en: B: Okay, I’ll wait for you at home.

ab: maru i pasamia u matata? hlatumua ku pakisuahlai ihlau kisuahlahlama Hla’alua na.
ch: A:你明天有空嗎?我想請問你關於拉阿魯哇的傳說故事。
en: A: Are you free tomorrow? I would like to ask you about the legendary story of Hla’alua.

ab: muasa ku vuvulunga matata, uka’a ku salia.
ch: B:明天我要去山上，不在家喔。
en: B: I’m going to the mountains tomorrow, so I won’t be home.

ab: hlitumimahla ku macahlia u ami sitakuamia Hla’alua na, hlatumua patuturu ihlau.
ch: A:我聽說您對拉阿魯哇的傳統習俗非常了解，想要請教您一下。
en: A: I heard that you are very familiar with the traditional customs of La Aloua and I would like to ask you a few questions.

ab: kuatumuhlu acahli ku, ucanika tuahlʉ aku umalivalʉ.
ch: B:我所了解的不多，但是我會盡量回答。
en: B: I don’t know much about it, but I will try to answer as best I can.

ab: pakisuahlai tuahli ku i pi’ahlingu? aunana ia cucuari matalakiahlʉ sumuhlatʉ.
ch: A:請問我可以錄影嗎?這樣以後比較容易記錄。
en: A: Can I record the video? It will be easier to record in the future.

ab: a’ʉ, ku pi pahluamia.
en: B: Okay, no problem.

ab: pakisuahlai taininicu a caihli u mamisa?
en: A: How old are you?

ab: masiahli cu ualu a caihli ku.
en: B: I’m 98 years old.

ab: tamuucu alamurumana.
en: A: You’re so young.

ab: uka’a, hlamama cua ku.
en: B: No, I’m not! I’m old.

ab: pakisuahlai iniu hliangahli?
en: A: Where are you from?

ab: hliangahli a ku Rʉhlʉcʉ.
en: B: I’m from high school.

ab: pakisuahlai usumaniu na Hla’alua i mʉmʉa?
ch: A:請問您的太太也是拉阿魯哇族人嗎?
en: A: Is your wife also a La’arruvian?

ab: ku, kana’ana ia cucutahlana pinan.
en: B: No, she is a Puyuma.

ab: macahlia u i masi kari usumanʉ?
en: A: What languages do you speak?

ab: macahlia ku masi kari Hla’alua hla kari pinan.
ch: B: 我會說拉阿魯哇語和卑南語。
en: B: I speak Hla’alua and Puyuma.

ab: tam masasangarʉ arinani takuliliungu ihlau, kisu’alivata ihlau sitakuamia palisia siuruacʉka a’ʉ i ia?
ch: A:今天很高興能訪問您，請您談一下，有關狩獵的禁忌好嗎?
en: A: It’s a pleasure to interview you today, can you talk about the taboo of hunting?

ab: a’ʉ, aku ku alamuru mana rumahlaʉ karʉkʉhlʉ musa vuvulunga mʉrʉcʉka.
ch: B:好的。我年輕的時候很常上山打獵。
en: B: Yes. I used to go hunting in the mountains when I was young.

ab: ’ʉ’ʉraisa, hliturua u arinani ia tam mavacangʉ.
ch: A:謝謝，您今天所教導的真是太棒了。
en: A: Thank you, what you taught me today was really great.

ab: hlialulira ku rianʉ macuisa, hlipatailia ahlama.
ch: B:我所説的都是千真萬確的，祖先所流傳的講述。
en: B: All that I say is true, the sayings of my ancestors.

ab: sipakisuahlai a arinani miungu kani’i. sia’ʉ’ʉraisamana hlikihlahlamua u.
ch: A:今天的訪談就到這邊。非常謝謝您的分享。
en: A: That’s all for today’s interview. Thank you very much for sharing with us.

ab: kukia arinu, arupakiahlahlamu ita mana’ai.
en: B: You’re welcome, let’s talk again.

ab: miacikiricu a ku, ahli’ai mungahlangahla tumatumuhlu kisuahlahlama.
ch: A:我要離開了，期待下次聽到更多的故事。
en: A: I’m going to leave now and look forward to hearing more stories next time.

ab: a’ʉ, muasaʉlahlʉkia.
en: B: Okay! Please take care.
